evals.expected_output: Extracts activity ID, runs intervals_icu_api.py with --use-athlete-profile, provides structured coaching analysis with execution rating, interval review, and next session recommendation
expectations: Runs intervals_icu_api.py with --activity flag and the extracted ID
Output contains an execution rating (pass, warn, or fail)
Output contains an interval review section with per-lap breakdown
Asks for RPE (1-10) at the end of the analysis

evals.prompt: I have 4 hours this week for cycling training. What should I do?
evals.expected_output: Asks about current FTP/goals or uses known values, provides structured weekly plan using Training Recommendation template with specific sessions and power targets
expectations: Output uses the Training Recommendation template format
Sessions include power targets in both watts and %FTP
Total session time fits within the 4-hour constraint

evals.prompt: Build me a 60-minute sweet spot workout for Zwift
evals.expected_output: Generates .zwo file with warmup, sweet spot intervals at 88-94% FTP, cooldown; provides Workout summary template with TSS estimate and execution notes
expectations: Generates a .zwo file using generate_zwo.py or writes XML directly
Workout includes warmup, main set, and cooldown sections
Main set power targets are in the 88-94% FTP (sweet spot) range
Output includes a TSS estimate

evals.prompt: I just did a 20-minute FTP test, here's the link: https://intervals.icu/activities/i126468500
evals.expected_output: Detects FTP test via ftp_test field, calculates new FTP (20min avg x 0.95), compares to current FTP, recommends zone updates if change > 3%
expectations: Detects FTP test via ftp_test field in script output
Calculates estimated new FTP as 20min average x 0.95
Compares new FTP to current FTP value
Recommends zone update if change exceeds 3%

evals.prompt: How did my outdoor ride go? https://intervals.icu/activities/i126468510
evals.expected_output: Checks data_warnings for estimated power, caveats all power-based analysis, focuses feedback on HR, RPE, duration, and cadence instead
expectations: Checks data_warnings array in script output
Caveats or disclaims power-based metrics if power is estimated
Shifts analysis focus to HR, RPE, duration, or cadence

evals.prompt: Did I keep my recovery ride easy enough? https://intervals.icu/activities/i126468520
evals.expected_output: Checks zone distribution for Z1 dominance, flags any Z3+ time, assesses recovery quality based on HR and power staying below 55% FTP
expectations: Checks zone distribution for Z1/Z2 dominance
Flags any time spent in Z3 or above
References the 55% FTP threshold for recovery rides

evals.expected_output: Fetches recent activities via --list-recent, assesses training load and consistency trends, provides analysis and recommendations
expectations: Fetches recent activities via --list-recent or --weekly-summary
Provides assessment of training load or consistency trends

evals.prompt: how did my ride go today
evals.expected_output: Asks for intervals.icu link or fetches most recent activity, runs analysis, provides coaching feedback
expectations: Either asks for an intervals.icu link or uses --latest to fetch most recent activity
Provides structured coaching feedback

evals.prompt: What power should I hold for tempo intervals?
evals.expected_output: References training zones, provides Z3 tempo range in both %FTP and watts based on current FTP
expectations: References Z3 tempo zone boundaries (75-90% FTP)
Provides power range in both %FTP and absolute watts

evals.prompt: Compare my last two threshold workouts
evals.expected_output: Fetches recent activities, identifies threshold sessions, runs analysis on both, compares power, HR, consistency, and adaptation signals
expectations: Fetches and analyzes at least two activities
Compares power, HR, or consistency metrics between the sessions

evals.expected_output: Runs pmc_calculator.py --bootstrap for PMC baseline, designs periodized block using periodization.md rules, writes active_plan.md, generates week 1 .zwo files via batch_generate_zwo.py
expectations: Runs pmc_calculator.py --bootstrap for PMC baseline
Reads references/periodization.md for block design rules
Writes plans/active_plan.md with the plan structure
Generates week 1 .zwo files via batch_generate_zwo.py

evals.prompt: How did week 1 go? Let's review my training week
evals.expected_output: Runs pmc_calculator.py --weekly-update with plan context, compares planned vs actual TSS, applies adaptation decision trees from periodization.md, proposes adjustments for next week
expectations: Runs pmc_calculator.py --weekly-update
Compares planned vs actual TSS in a table
Applies adaptation decision trees from periodization.md
Proposes adjustments for the next week

evals.prompt: What's my plan status? What workout is next?
evals.expected_output: Reads plans/active_plan.md, shows current week schedule with completed/upcoming workouts, identifies next session with power targets and focus
expectations: Reads plans/active_plan.md for current state
Shows current week schedule with completion status
Identifies the next session with power targets
